sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to break off, to break in pieces, to separate by breaking. This is the original power of the bilateral פר, compare פָּרַר, פָּרַם, פָּרַשׂ, פָּרַץ, פָּרַק, פָּרַם, which are variously applied to the significations of dispersing (פָּרַט, פָּרַץ), letting go (פָּרַע), breaking forth (פָּרַח), expanding (פָּרַד, פָּרַשׁ, פַּרְשַׁז), and also that of judging (פָּרַז). Compare as to the power of the similar syllable בר, under the word בָּרָא.

(2) to expand, e.g. wings, Eze 1:11 whence Syr. ܦܪܰܕ to fly, to flee away. Compare פֶּרֶד.

(3) to scatter, i.q. פָּרַט; whence פְּרֻדֹות.

Niphal-

(1) to separate oneself, 2Sa 1:23 followed by מִן Jud. 4:11 , and מֵעַל from any one, Gen 13:9, seq. Part. נִפְרָד one separating himself from others, one who despises others, one who lives only for himself (Sonderling), Pro 18:1.

(2) to scatter selves, to be divided, Gen 10:5, 32 Gen 10:32(compare 25:23 , and נָפַץ 9:19 ); Neh 4:13.

Piel, intrans. to go aside (for fornication), Hos 4:14. (Arab. فَرَّدَ to go aside for purposes of devotion.)

Pual, part. to be separated, singular (see Niphal, Pro 18:1), Est 3:8.

Hiphil-

(1) to separate, Gen 30:40 Pro 16:28 17:9 followed by בֵּין (compare הִבְדִּיל בֵּין) Rth 1:17 2Ki 2:11.

(2) to disperse, Deu 32:8.

Hithpael-

(1) to separate oneself, to be put asunder, Job 41:9 Psa 22:15.

(2) to be dispersed, Job 4:11.

Derived nouns. פְּרֻדָה … פֶּרֶד, and pr.n. פְּרוּדָא.
